title: Before 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple, Nia DaCosta Took Over Another Iconic Horror Franchise
text: Nia DaCosta burst onto the filmmaking scene in 2018 with her directorial debut Little Woods, which earned critical acclaim. That same year, Jordan Peele hired her to direct a Candyman sequel, a significant opportunity for a filmmaker just starting her career. DaCosta co-wrote and directed the 2021 Candyman with Peele and Win Rosenfeld. The film succeeded by honoring the original's themes rather than simply recreating it. She explained her approach: There is definitely a sense of taking ownership, and telling a Black story about Black people. The reimagining featured Yahya Abdul-Mateen II as a new Candyman, which worked brilliantly because the character functions as a legend built on Black tragedies. The film incorporated social commentary on gentrification and police brutality while delivering genuine scares. It earned $77 million worldwide and strong critical reviews. Her Candyman success positioned DaCosta to direct 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple (2026), the sequel to Danny Boyle's acclaimed film. She maintained her independent vision, stating she didn't want to make a Danny Boyle movie. The film is receiving spectacular reviews for her distinctive directorial spin.
